verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 belongs to the IZUMO family of sperm-egg fusion-associated proteins. Unlike IZUMO1 (clear single-pass TM with Ig-like ectodomain on sperm surface), IZUMO4 lacks a TM domain and is thought to form disulfide-linked complexes with other IZUMO family members on the sperm surface during the acrosome reaction. NCBI notes nuclear localization, but family biology and signal-peptide architecture point to germline-restricted surface display via stable association with TM IZUMO partners on acrosome-reacted sperm. This is narrow germline-lineage display, not baseline somatic surface residency, so contextual/tissue_restricted_surface fits.
predicted_key_uncertainty: NCBI calls it nuclear; surface display via IZUMO-complex tethering on sperm is inferred from family biology rather than direct IZUMO4-specific surface proteomics.
